commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1ad568ba1e0db9051904eacee025ee36ec2f06ee)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ACP stale-session helpers and changes ACP dispatch to unbind bound conversations on generic ACP init failure when persisted ACP metadata points to a missing cwd.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +79. Total +129 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main and v2026.6.6 only unbind selected stale ACP error messages and do not check persisted cwd on generic init failure. I did not run a live current-main repro, but the PR includes live after-fix staging logs for the same dispatch cleanup path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the branch is dirty against current main and the remaining decision is session-state cleanup acceptance, not a narrow autonomous repair.

target.title: fix(acp): treat missing cwd as stale bound session
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: bound ACP sessions can stay attached after the persisted runtime working directory disappears, so retries keep failing against a dead session.
- Why it matters: affected conversations remain wedged until stale binding state is manually cleared.
- What changed: share ACP stale-session cleanup and, during ACP dispatch, treat a generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` as stale only when the bound ACP session metadata resolves to a missing persisted `cwd`.
- Scope boundary: this current head is dispatch-only. Earlier reset-in-place changes were removed because current `main` no longer appears to route the relevant bound `/new` or `/reset` path through that helper.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- ACP stale-session classification helper
- Auto-reply ACP dispatch stale-binding cleanup
- Handler-level regression for generic ACP init failure when persisted cwd is missing
- Test-only compatibility shim already present on this branch for the current `memory-core` subagent runtime mock contract

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #60720
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

The dispatch path already removes stale ACP bindings for selected ACP session init failures. The live missing-cwd case can surface as a generic spawn `ENOENT` wrapped as `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, while the reliable stale signal is in the bound ACP session metadata: its persisted `runtimeOptions.cwd` no longer exists. Without checking that metadata, the stale bound conversation can survive the failed dispatch and keep routing future turns into the dead ACP session.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` covers generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` when the persisted ACP cwd is missing and asserts that the stale session key is unbound.
- Existing stale-binding tests still cover specific ACP stale-session errors.
- The classifier stays narrow: cleanup requires either the existing stale ACP error classification or `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` plus a missing persisted ACP cwd.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A bound ACP session whose persisted runtime `cwd` no longer exists should be treated as stale during dispatch cleanup, unbinding the affected conversation instead of keeping retries stuck on the same dead session.
- Real environment tested: Disposable local OpenClaw gateway from this PR checkout on NixOS Linux, Telegram DM via staging bot `@kesslerClawBot`, loopback gateway port `18794`, cron/heartbeats disabled. Gateway was stopped after the proof run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the disposable staging gateway from PR head `1ad568ba1e0db9051904eacee025ee36ec2f06ee`, sent a Telegram DM to `@kesslerClawBot` that routed through a bound ACP conversation whose persisted `runtimeOptions.cwd` pointed at `/home/art/.local/state/openclaw-staging/disposable/missing-cwd-pr68843`, let ACP dispatch fail with generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, then inspected the post-run binding state.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted public proof artifact: https://gist.github.com/kesslerio/dbefdac0c5d5a5849997b6bf702047c6. Follow-up proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68843#issuecomment-4558022819.

  ```txt
  2026-05-27T12:18:57.491-07:00 telegram: routed via bound conversation 8352721935 -> agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1
  2026-05-27T12:18:57.520-07:00 telegram inbound: chatId=8352721935 ... preview="[...] PR68843 patched missing cwd cleanup proof"
  2026-05-27T12:19:06.365-07:00 [plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend ready
  2026-05-27T12:19:06.400-07:00 dispatch-acp: removed 1 stale bound conversation(s) for agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1 after ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: Failed to spawn agent command: /run/current-system/sw/bin/codex-acp-openclaw
  2026-05-27T12:19:06.404-07:00 acp-dispatch: session=agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1 outcome=error code=ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED latencyMs=327 queueDepth=0 activeRuntimes=0
  ```

  Post-run binding state:

  ```json
  {
    "bindingCount": 0,
    "sessionKey": "agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1",
    "acpState": "error",
    "runtimeOptions": {
      "cwd": "/home/art/.local/state/openclaw-staging/disposable/missing-cwd-pr68843"
    }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the staging Telegram DM routed through the stale bound ACP conversation, ACPX reached the runtime backend, dispatch failed with generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, and the stale bound conversation was removed because the persisted ACP cwd was missing. Post-run binding state showed `bindingCount: 0`.
- What was not tested: Production AlphaClaw was not used. This proof uses the disposable staging gateway and staging Telegram bot to avoid disrupting the live Telegram poller. A broader channel-neutral ACP proof was not run because the Telegram staging proof exercises the dispatch cleanup path directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the raw local `/home/art/...` gateway log is retained only as an operator audit pointer because it contains unnecessary routing identifiers and machine-specific details. Public proof is the redacted gist and copied transcript above.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current `main` only treated selected ACP init-failure messages as stale and did not inspect persisted ACP session `cwd` metadata for a generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`; ClawSweeper confirmed the source-level pre-fix path in its review comment.

## Tests and validation

Commands run on head `1ad568ba1e0db9051904eacee025ee36ec2f06ee`:

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
pnpm vitest src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts -t "persisted cwd is missing"
pnpm vitest src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts -t "stale bindings|persisted cwd is missing|missing-cwd"
```

Results:

```txt
oxfmt: Finished in 121ms on 3 files using 12 threads.
focused persisted-cwd test: 1 passed, 51 skipped
focused stale-binding dispatch suite: 4 passed, 48 skipped
```

A duplicate local retry of the same focused Vitest selection later hung during runner startup and was stopped with a timeout; the clean focused run above completed successfully after the patch.

CI proof gate previously failed because this proof was only in comments / non-parseable body fields. This body now uses the exact fields parsed by `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`.

## Security Impact

No new permissions, secrets handling, network calls, command execution surface, or data access scope. The behavioral expansion is limited to unbinding stale ACP conversations after an ACP init failure when the persisted ACP cwd is confirmed missing.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config or migration required. The change affects only stale ACP init failure recovery.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted refresh, narrowing, validation, staging proof, and PR-body update.
target.author: kesslerio

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
